pi_items.source: setup_task.sh creates the file dynamically at this exact path (line 16)
task.json description (line 9) specifies saving as 'aurora3_bdd.drawio' in '~/Diagrams' directory
task.json description (line 10) specifies exporting as 'aurora3_bdd.pdf' in '~/Diagrams' directory
setup_task.sh specification file lists all 5 blocks (lines 28-57): Power Subsystem (root), Solar Panel, Battery Module, EPS Mainboard, Wiring Harness

setup_task.sh specification file contains all properties: efficiency (Solar Panel, line 17), capacity (Battery Module, line 25), voltage (Battery Module line 26, EPS Mainboard line 49), mass (EPS Mainboard line 33, Wiring Harness line 39)

setup_task.sh specification file specifies: Solar Panel multiplicity 4 (lines 31, 36), Battery Module multiplicity 1 (lines 39, 44), EPS Mainboard multiplicity 1 (lines 47, 51), Wiring Harness multiplicity 1 (lines 54, 57)

privileged_info_summary: The Aurora-3 Power Subsystem is a synthetic engineering specification created for SysML training purposes. The specification includes a hierarchical system with a root 'Power Subsystem' block and four child blocks: Solar Panel (4 units), Battery Module (1 unit), EPS Mainboard (1 unit), and Wiring Harness (1 unit). All components must be connected via SysML composition relationships (solid diamond connectors). Required value properties to include are mass, capacity, efficiency, and voltage. Solar Panel: efficiency 29.5%, area 0.03 m2. Battery Module: capacity 40 Wh, voltage 14.8 V. EPS Mainboard: rails 3.3V/5V/12V, mass 0.25 kg. Wiring Harness: mass 0.05 kg. The specification is created at runtime in /home/ga/Desktop/aurora3_power_spec.txt, with deliverables expected at /home/ga/Diagrams/aurora3_bdd.drawio and /home/ga/Diagrams/aurora3_bdd.pdf.
